module: RUM, Mobile Monitoring, and Session Replay
feature: Inventory and dependency graph: browser SDK / cost
user_story: As an operator, I can understand and act on browser SDK cost posture without leaving Mayyam.
ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for browser SDK: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Discover browser SDK, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the cost pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for browser SDK.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for browser SDK.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for RUM, Mobile Monitoring, and Session Replay browser SDK inventory with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: browser-sdk_cost_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for browser SDK cost that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for browser SDK; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for browser SDK.
cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for browser SDK, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for browser SDK.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for browser SDK.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing browser SDK data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling browser SDK inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for browser SDK.
acceptance_criteria: Given browser SDK data exists, when the cost inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: browser SDK connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

user_story: As an operator, I can understand and act on page load timing security posture without leaving Mayyam.
ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for page load timing: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Protect page load timing security behavior with unit, integration, fixture, and UI tests.
backend_scope: Add fixtures, fake clients, unit tests, and API contract tests for page load timing.
frontend_scope: Add component and e2e tests for page load timing critical flows.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for RUM, Mobile Monitoring, and Session Replay page load timing tests with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: page-load-timing_security_tests evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for page load timing security that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for page load timing; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for page load timing.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for page load timing recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for page load timing.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for page load timing.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing page load timing data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling page load timing tests broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for page load timing.
acceptance_criteria: Given page load timing data exists, when the security tests workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: page load timing connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for Core Web Vitals: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Summarize Core Web Vitals evidence, likely causes, confidence, uncertainty, and next diagnostic commands for security issues.
backend_scope: Add context builder, prompt template, evidence citations, and LLM safety constraints for Core Web Vitals.
frontend_scope: Add triage panel, follow-up prompts, saved notes, and feedback loop for Core Web Vitals.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for RUM, Mobile Monitoring, and Session Replay Core Web Vitals ai-triage with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: core-web-vitals_security_ai-triage evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for Core Web Vitals security that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for Core Web Vitals; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for Core Web Vitals.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for Core Web Vitals recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for Core Web Vitals.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for Core Web Vitals.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing Core Web Vitals data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling Core Web Vitals ai-triage broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for Core Web Vitals.
acceptance_criteria: Given Core Web Vitals data exists, when the security ai-triage workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: Core Web Vitals connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

user_story: As an operator, I can understand and act on funnel analysis disaster-recovery posture without leaving Mayyam.
ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for funnel analysis: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Summarize funnel analysis evidence, likely causes, confidence, uncertainty, and next diagnostic commands for disaster-recovery issues.
backend_scope: Add context builder, prompt template, evidence citations, and LLM safety constraints for funnel analysis.
frontend_scope: Add triage panel, follow-up prompts, saved notes, and feedback loop for funnel analysis.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for RUM, Mobile Monitoring, and Session Replay funnel analysis ai-triage with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: funnel-analysis_disaster-recovery_ai-triage evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for funnel analysis disaster-recovery that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for funnel analysis; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for funnel analysis.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for funnel analysis recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for funnel analysis.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for funnel analysis.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing funnel analysis data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling funnel analysis ai-triage broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for funnel analysis.
acceptance_criteria: Given funnel analysis data exists, when the disaster-recovery ai-triage workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: funnel analysis connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for mobile crash reporting: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Let an agent investigate mobile crash reporting security issues with bounded tool use, read-only diagnostics first, explicit uncertainty, and approval-gated mutations.
backend_scope: Add tool registry, planner, execution trace, read-only default policy, token/time budgets, rollback plan schema, and stop conditions for mobile crash reporting.
frontend_scope: Add investigation timeline, tool-call evidence, confidence labels, approval prompts, replay, and promote-to-runbook actions for mobile crash reporting.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for RUM, Mobile Monitoring, and Session Replay mobile crash reporting agentic-investigation with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: mobile-crash-reporting_security_agentic-investigation evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for mobile crash reporting security that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for mobile crash reporting; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for mobile crash reporting.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for mobile crash reporting recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for mobile crash reporting.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for mobile crash reporting.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing mobile crash reporting data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling mobile crash reporting agentic-investigation broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for mobile crash reporting.
acceptance_criteria: Given mobile crash reporting data exists, when the security agentic-investigation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: mobile crash reporting connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for mobile network telemetry: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Protect mobile network telemetry cost behavior with unit, integration, fixture, and UI tests.
backend_scope: Add fixtures, fake clients, unit tests, and API contract tests for mobile network telemetry.
frontend_scope: Add component and e2e tests for mobile network telemetry critical flows.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for RUM, Mobile Monitoring, and Session Replay mobile network telemetry tests with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: mobile-network-telemetry_cost_tests evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for mobile network telemetry cost that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for mobile network telemetry; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for mobile network telemetry.
cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for mobile network telemetry, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for mobile network telemetry.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for mobile network telemetry.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing mobile network telemetry data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling mobile network telemetry tests broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for mobile network telemetry.
acceptance_criteria: Given mobile network telemetry data exists, when the cost tests workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: mobile network telemetry connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for mobile network telemetry: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Detect mobile network telemetry symptoms, thresholds, anomalies, and correlated events that affect security.
backend_scope: Add deterministic evaluators, metrics ingestion, and explainable findings for mobile network telemetry.
frontend_scope: Add health badges, charts, timelines, and affected-object drilldowns for mobile network telemetry.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for RUM, Mobile Monitoring, and Session Replay mobile network telemetry health with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: mobile-network-telemetry_security_health evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for mobile network telemetry security that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for mobile network telemetry; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for mobile network telemetry.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for mobile network telemetry recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for mobile network telemetry.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for mobile network telemetry.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing mobile network telemetry data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling mobile network telemetry health broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for mobile network telemetry.
acceptance_criteria: Given mobile network telemetry data exists, when the security health workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: mobile network telemetry connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging
